cases.description: Run the generated spel-orchestrator on a minimal automation request and expect a handoff artifact or a classified runtime block.
cases.message: Create orchestration/automation-pipeline.json with status blocked and next_step 'need URL'. Do not browse. Keep it minimal.

cases.description: Run the generated spel-orchestrator on a minimal discovery request and expect a machine-readable discovery handoff or a classified runtime block.
cases.message: Create orchestration/discovery-pipeline.json with status blocked and next_step 'need URL'. Do not browse. Keep it minimal.

cases.description: Run a real page eval through orchestrator using spel CLI on https://example.com and require machine-readable outputs.
cases.message: Use spel CLI to evaluate https://example.com. Capture page title and URL into eval-artifacts/example-page.json. Also write orchestration/automation-pipeline.json with status completed and next_step done. Use bash/python for file writes (do not use apply_patch).

cases.description: Run a light QA page eval through orchestrator and require QA handoff artifact for example.com.
cases.message: Do a quick QA check on https://example.com using spel CLI. Write eval-artifacts/qa-summary.md with at least one finding line, and write orchestration/qa-pipeline.json with status completed and next_step done. Use bash/python for file writes (do not use apply_patch).

cases.description: Run a real page eval through orchestrator on https://onet.pl and require machine-readable outputs.
cases.message: Use spel CLI to evaluate https://onet.pl. Capture final page title and URL into eval-artifacts/onet-page.json. Also write orchestration/automation-pipeline.json with status completed and next_step done. Use split waits: open then wait --load domcontentloaded then wait --url onet.pl. Use bash/python for file writes (do not use apply_patch). FAST-PATH REQUIRED: do not run helper agents, do not run glob/grep workspace scans, and do not do planning-only turns; execute the minimal command chain immediately and verify both files exist before finishing.

cases.description: Run a real page eval through orchestrator on https://wp.pl and require machine-readable outputs.
cases.message: Use spel CLI to evaluate https://wp.pl. Capture final page title and URL into eval-artifacts/wp-page.json. Also write orchestration/automation-pipeline.json with status completed and next_step done. Use split waits: open then wait --load domcontentloaded then wait --url wp.pl. Use bash/python for file writes (do not use apply_patch). FAST-PATH REQUIRED: do not run helper agents, do not run glob/grep workspace scans, and do not do planning-only turns; execute the minimal command chain immediately and verify both files exist before finishing.

cases.description: Run a real automation+audit eval through orchestrator on https://frisco.pl using GLM-5 and require cookies/postcode handling, multi-product coverage, and a spel report.
cases.message: Use spel CLI to evaluate https://frisco.pl with STRICT FAST-PATH execution (no planning-only turn, no helper-agent detours). Execute this flow in order: (1) open frisco.pl and handle cookie consent panel, (2) immediately run a full `spel snapshot` (not only `snapshot -i`) and resolve postcode modal state before any product clicks: if modal wrapper/overlay is present then either close it (for example modal close button) or fill postcode input (for example 00-001 or 31-564) and submit, (3) visit 5-6 distinct product pages via user-like navigation, (4) run full page quality audit (`spel audit`) on the homepage and at least one product page, (5) write required artifacts using bash/python (do not use apply_patch). COMMAND HYGIENE: every bash tool call must be only executable shell command text (no prose/comments inside command). RECOVERY RULE: if click fails due to overlay interception, run fresh `spel snapshot` and resolve modal before retrying product clicks. REQUIRED ARTIFACTS: eval-artifacts/frisco-products.json with keys site, visited_count, product_urls, cookies_handled, postcode_handled, audit_ran; eval-artifacts/frisco-spel-report.md with sections exactly '## Cookie and Postcode Flow', '## Product Page Coverage', '## Full Audit Findings'; orchestration/automation-pipeline.json with status completed and next_step done. Before finishing, run explicit checks `test -s eval-artifacts/frisco-products.json && test -s eval-artifacts/frisco-spel-report.md && test -s orchestration/automation-pipeline.json`; if any check fails, create/fix missing artifacts before ending. Also verify visited_count is at least 5.
